definition_en: A custom or belief passed down through generations
example: It is a tradition to eat turkey on Thanksgiving.

definition_en: The history, culture and characteristics inherited from the past
example: The city is proud of its architectural heritage.

definition_en: Showing great variety; including many different types
example: London is one of the most culturally diverse cities in the world.

definition_en: A set of actions performed in a fixed order, often for religious or cultural reasons
example: The wedding ritual varies across different cultures.

definition_en: An oversimplified and generalised image of a particular type of person or thing
example: It is important to challenge cultural stereotypes.

definition_en: A group of people of a different race, religion or culture from the majority
example: The rights of ethnic minorities must be protected.

definition_en: Traditional stories, customs and beliefs of a community
example: The festival celebrates the folklore of the local indigenous community.

definition_en: The process by which a person or group adopts the culture of another group
example: Immigrants often face pressure to assimilate into the host culture.

definition_en: Including or relating to many different cultures
example: Australia is a multicultural society with people from all over the world.

definition_en: The rules of polite behaviour in society or a profession
example: Table etiquette varies greatly between cultures.

definition_en: A form of a language spoken by people from a particular region
example: She speaks with a strong northern dialect.

definition_en: Originating from or naturally occurring in a particular place
example: Indigenous communities are working to preserve their languages.

definition_en: A formal event with set actions marking an important occasion
example: The graduation ceremony was held in the main hall.

definition_en: Something considered socially unacceptable to discuss or do
example: Discussing money is still a taboo in some cultures.

definition_en: An unfair opinion formed without enough knowledge or thought
example: Prejudice against immigrants continues to be a serious problem.

definition_en: The process by which the world becomes more connected through trade and culture
example: Globalisation has made cultural exchange faster than ever before.

definition_en: A journey to a place considered sacred or special
example: Millions make a pilgrimage to Mecca each year.

definition_en: Able to speak two languages fluently
example: Being bilingual opens many career opportunities.

definition_en: Accepted ways of behaving in a particular society or group
example: It is important to respect local customs when travelling.

definition_en: A collection of traditional stories and myths from a culture or religion
example: Greek mythology has had a huge influence on Western literature.

definition_en: A person from whom you are descended
example: She traced her ancestors back to Ireland.

definition_en: Relating to communication or interaction between different cultures
example: Intercultural skills are essential in international business.
